children.text: projects that facilitate the transition of children and youth between State-operated institutions, or institutions in the State operated by the Secretary of the Interior, and schools served by local educational agencies or schools operated or funded by the Bureau of Indian Education; or

children.label: A
children.text: preplacement programs that allow adjudicated or incarcerated youth to audit or attend courses on college, university, or community college campuses, or through programs provided in institutional settings;

children.label: B
children.text: worksite schools, in which institutions of higher education and private or public employers partner to create programs to help students make a successful transition to postsecondary education and employment; and

children.label: i
children.text: personal, career and technical, and academic, counseling;

children.label: ii
children.text: placement services designed to place the youth in a university, college, or junior college program;

children.label: iii
children.text: information concerning, and assistance in obtaining, available student financial aid;

children.heading: Conduct of projects
children.label: b Conduct of projects
children.text: A project supported under this section may be conducted directly by the State agency, or through a contract or other arrangement with one or more local educational agencies, other public agencies, or private nonprofit organizations.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: c Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to prohibit a school that receives funds under subsection (a) from serving neglected and delinquent children and youth simultaneously with students with similar educational needs, in the same educational settings where appropriate.
